title: Surface Config
description: The RUNTIME-editable config for a world surface, keyed by (world_slug, surface). Distinct from world-manifest.schema.json (the INFRA declaration): the manifest declares hosting/identity/secrets/tenant_id/guild_ids (deploy-time, terraform-bound), this declares per-surface runtime content a community-manager edits live (copy, theme). The two compose on the same per-world model — surface-config NEVER duplicates the manifest's tenant_id/guild_ids/auth (those stay the manifest's source-of-truth; surface-config references world_slug only). C-1 extraction: ports Jani's sietch Theme model (themes/sietch/src/ui/builder/src/types/index.ts) + the head-pointer/append-only/optimistic-lock machinery (themes/sietch/src/services/config/ConfigService.ts) into freeside-worlds as a config service. V1 ships the `verify-message` surface only; the shape is generic (surface -> validated JSON), NOT a table per surface. Validated by Ajv at the service boundary, mirroring packages/registry/bin/validate.ts. Schema governance per packages/protocol/VERSIONING.md (enum-locked schema_version, additive-only minor bumps).

schema_version.description: Schema version. Enum-locked per VERSIONING.md. v1.0 (cycle-C C-1 · 2026-05-30) ships the verify-message surface + generic surface->JSON envelope.

world_slug.description: World identifier. MUST match an existing world-manifest `slug` (packages/registry/worlds/<slug>.yaml). This is a REFERENCE to the manifest's world, not a redeclaration — surface-config carries no tenant_id/guild_ids/auth (those live in the manifest). Same regex as world-manifest.schema.json `slug`.

surface.description: The world surface this config targets. Enum-locked (additive minor bumps add surfaces). v1.0: `verify-message` only (the community-manager-editable verify-flow message). Future surfaces (landing-copy, role-grant-message, etc.) are additive enum extensions per VERSIONING.md.

config.description: The validated, surface-specific config payload. Discriminated by `surface` via the allOf below. Stored as opaque JSONB in the store's head pointer + history; validated against the per-surface shape at the service boundary BEFORE write (fail-closed on write) and is trusted on read (fail-soft: 404 -> caller uses defaults).

allOf.description: Per-surface config discrimination. `verify-message` config MUST match VerifyMessageConfig.

VerifyMessageConfig.description: V1 community-manager-editable verify-message surface. `enabled` toggles the surface; `copy` is the displayed text; `theme` is the OPTIONAL Jani Theme override (omitted -> caller uses the world's default theme).

enabled.description: Whether the verify-message surface is active. Fail-soft default when the whole config 404s is `true` with default copy (caller decides); this field lets a CM disable the surface without deleting the config.

copy.description: The editable text shown on the verify surface.

theme.description: OPTIONAL theme override (Jani's sietch Theme model). Omit to inherit the world's default theme.

Theme.description: Jani's sietch Theme model, ported verbatim from themes/sietch/src/ui/builder/src/types/index.ts (interface Theme). Fields: id, name, description?, branding, pages, createdAt, updatedAt. `createdAt`/`updatedAt` are ISO-8601 strings (sietch stored them as `string`).

ThemeBranding.description: Jani's ThemeBranding (interface ThemeBranding). colors (6 named), fonts (heading/body family+weight), borderRadius enum, spacing enum.

PageLayout.description: Jani's PageLayout (interface PageLayout). id, name, slug, components[].

ComponentInstance.description: Jani's ComponentInstance (interface ComponentInstance) — recursive tree. id, type, props (open record), children? (recursive).

props.description: Open record (sietch: Record<string, unknown>). Component-specific props validated by the component's own schema, not here.
